bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +37.2% YoY in FY2026 Q2 to $3.62B, with TTM revenue of $12.74B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.

Profitability premium — net margin of 32.5% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +15.5pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.

Management credibility — MCS of 0.80 on 6 tracked forward claims (4B/0H/1M record). When management says they will deliver, they typically do — a meaningful re-rating support that is often overlooked in quantitative screens.

Trend regime constructive — spot $398.05 sits +35.8% above the 200-DMA ($293.05). The long-term moving average has been a reliable reference for institutional capital and remains supportive of the directional bias.

Options market complacent — front-month implied move of just 4.3% suggests the market is not pricing in a binary outcome at the next print. Asymmetric setup if the company delivers a positive surprise — limited downside priced, full upside available.
bear: Put skew elevated — ATM put IV running +2.9pp above call IV. The options market is paying up for downside protection — a contrarian green-flag in extreme cases, but more often a leading indicator of impending news.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 40% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 34.0% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 34% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 30.5% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $210.47 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line:
BULLISH. Analog Devices, Inc. (ADI) printed $3.62B in revenue in FY2026 Q2 (+37.2% YoY), net margin of 32.5%. Management credibility is strong (MCS 0.80, 4B/0H/1M record), supporting take-up of the forward guide. At 29.5x forward P/E, the stock carries a 23% premium to peers (24.0x median) — the multiple needs continued execution to defend.

Technical regime is constructive: spot $398.05 above the 200-DMA ($293.05, +35.8%) with neutral momentum (RSI 48). Sell-side consensus PT of $275 implies -30.9% upside. The options market is pricing a ±4.3% move by the front-month expiry — a complacent read on near-term risk. 30-day implied move is ±12.1%. Position-sizing should reflect conviction; use the trigger list to manage adds and trims around the next earnings catalyst.
